Fungal infection.description: A fungal infection caused by fungi that can affect the skin, nails, and mucous membranes.

Allergy.description: An immune system response to a foreign substance like pollen, pet dander, or certain foods.
precautions: Keep windows closed during pollen season

GERD.description: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease - chronic acid reflux causing heartburn and discomfort.

Chronic cholestasis.description: A condition where bile flow from the liver is reduced or blocked.

Drug Reaction.description: An adverse reaction to a medication causing various symptoms from mild rashes to severe conditions.

Peptic ulcer diseae.description: Sores that develop on the lining of the stomach, small intestine, or esophagus.

AIDS.description: Advanced stage of HIV infection that severely damages the immune system.

Diabetes.description: A metabolic disease causing high blood sugar due to insulin production or utilization issues.

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of stomach and intestines causing vomiting and diarrhea, often from viral infection.

Bronchial Asthma.description: A condition causing airways to swell and narrow, making breathing difficult.

Hypertension.description: High blood pressure that can lead to heart disease, stroke, and kidney problems.

Migraine.description: Severe recurring headaches often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and sensitivity to light.

Cervical spondylosis.description: Age-related wear and tear affecting spinal disks in the neck.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).description: Loss of muscle function due to brain hemorrhage affecting motor control.

Jaundice.description: Yellowing of skin and eyes due to high bilirubin levels in blood.

Malaria.description: A mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites affecting red blood cells.

Chicken pox.description: Highly contagious viral infection causing itchy blisters and flu-like symptoms.

Dengue.description: Mosquito-borne viral disease causing high fever, severe headache, and joint pain.
precautions: Seek medical attention for severe symptoms

Typhoid.description: Bacterial infection causing prolonged high fever, weakness, and stomach pain.

hepatitis A.description: Highly contagious liver infection caused by the hepatitis A virus.

Hepatitis B.description: Serious liver infection caused by hepatitis B virus, can become chronic.

Hepatitis C.description: A viral infection causing liver inflammation, often leading to serious liver damage.

Hepatitis D.description: A serious liver disease caused by hepatitis D virus, only occurs with hepatitis B.

Hepatitis E.description: A liver disease caused by hepatitis E virus, usually spreads through contaminated water.

Alcoholic hepatitis.description: Liver inflammation caused by drinking too much alcohol over time.

Tuberculosis.description: A serious bacterial infection that mainly affects the lungs and can spread through air.

Common Cold.description: A mild viral infection of the upper respiratory tract causing runny nose and cough.

Pneumonia.description: Infection causing inflammation of air sacs in lungs, which may fill with fluid.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).description: Swollen veins in the rectum or anus causing pain, bleeding, and discomfort.

Heart attack.description: Occurs when blood flow to part of the heart is blocked, causing heart muscle damage.

Varicose veins.description: Twisted, enlarged veins visible under the skin, usually in legs.

Hypothyroidism.description: A condition where the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough thyroid hormones.

Hyperthyroidism.description: Overactive thyroid gland producing excessive thyroid hormones.

Hypoglycemia.description: Abnormally low blood sugar level causing dizziness, confusion, and weakness.

Osteoarthristis.description: Degenerative joint disease causing cartilage breakdown and joint pain.

Arthritis.description: Inflammation of one or more joints causing pain, stiffness, and swelling.

(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.description: A condition causing brief episodes of dizziness due to inner ear crystals movement.

Acne.description: Skin condition causing pimples, blackheads, and cysts, especially on the face.

Urinary tract infection.description: Infection in any part of the urinary system including kidneys, bladder, and urethra.

Psoriasis.description: A chronic autoimmune condition causing rapid skin cell buildup creating scaling patches.

Impetigo.description: A highly contagious bacterial skin infection causing sores and blisters.
